codebase-question.name: Codebase Question
codebase-question.description: Research the codebase thoroughly to answer the user's question. Read relevant files, trace code paths, and check git history as needed to build a complete understanding. Report your findings inline in the conversation — do not create files, PRs, or branches. Once you have answered the question with sufficient depth and evidence, stop and wait in `needs_input` state. Do NOT archive yourself — the user will review your answer on the Zimmer homepage and archive the session themselves. If the user asks follow-up questions, answer them.

open-reviewed-green-pr.name: Open Reviewed Green PR
open-reviewed-green-pr.description: Unmerged PR is open, CI is confirmed green (use the wait-for-CI skill to block until CI passes or fails), and an independent fresh-eyes review of the PR has been performed and all of its feedback addressed (use wait-for-CI again after any fixes). Run that review through your runtime's in-process review path — the /code-review skill, Claude Code's native Task/Agent subagent, or Codex's native spawn_agent subagent — and do NOT spin up a separate Zimmer session solely to review your own PR (reserve start_session for genuinely independent downstream work). Ensure the PR title/description accurately reflects the final implementation. The PR description MUST include a '## Verification' section with checked boxes documenting how you closed the loop (CI green, tests added/passing, self-review done) AND proof that the change works (screenshots for UI changes, e2e test results for logic changes, external confirmation for side effects). For UI changes, follow the screenshot capture-upload-embed procedure in GIT_WORKFLOW.md. Do NOT use unchecked checkboxes or a 'Test plan' with aspirational TODOs — every verification item must be completed before the PR is opened. Once the PR is open, CI is green, and self-review is complete, report the PR URL and a summary of what changed, then STOP and wait in needs_input state. Do NOT archive yourself — open sessions with unreviewed PRs serve as a visible todo-list for the user in the Zimmer UI. Only the user (or an explicit follow-up message) should trigger archiving after they have reviewed the PR.

open-reviewed-green-pr-with-version-bump.name: Open Reviewed Green PR (with Version Bump)
open-reviewed-green-pr-with-version-bump.description: If your changes modify source code in any server's local/ or shared/ directories, you MUST invoke the /publish-and-pr skill with 'patch' (or 'minor'/'major' if appropriate) to do a version bump BEFORE finalizing your PR. This is non-negotiable — server source changes without a version bump leave npm packages out of sync with internal source code. The skill handles running tests, bumping the version, updating CHANGELOG.md, and staging all version-related files. After the version bump, proceed with the standard PR process: unmerged PR is open, CI is confirmed green (use the wait-for-CI skill to block until CI passes or fails), and an independent fresh-eyes review of the PR has been performed and all of its feedback addressed (use wait-for-CI again after any fixes). Run that review through your runtime's in-process review path — the /code-review skill, Claude Code's native Task/Agent subagent, or Codex's native spawn_agent subagent — and do NOT spin up a separate Zimmer session solely to review your own PR (reserve start_session for genuinely independent downstream work). Ensure the PR title/description accurately reflects the final implementation. The PR description MUST include a '## Verification' section with checked boxes documenting how you closed the loop (CI green, tests added/passing, self-review done, version bump completed) AND proof that the change works (screenshots for UI changes, e2e test results for logic changes, external confirmation for side effects). For UI changes, follow the screenshot capture-upload-embed procedure in GIT_WORKFLOW.md. Do NOT use unchecked checkboxes or a 'Test plan' with aspirational TODOs — every verification item must be completed before the PR is opened. Once the PR is open, CI is green, and self-review is complete, report the PR URL and a summary of what changed, then STOP and wait in needs_input state. Do NOT archive yourself — open sessions with unreviewed PRs serve as a visible todo-list for the user in the Zimmer UI. Only the user (or an explicit follow-up message) should trigger archiving after they have reviewed the PR.

e2e-verified-green-pr.name: E2E Verified Green PR
e2e-verified-green-pr.description: Before testing, explicitly identify and state the critical path(s) of your change in your response — the specific code path, feature, or behavior you modified (e.g., 'I changed how air prepare is invoked, so the critical path is: trigger a session that runs air prepare and verify the new invocation works'). Use the start dev server skill to spin up a local instance of the environment. Test the critical path first: trigger the specific workflow, endpoint, or UI flow that your change affects and confirm it behaves correctly. Then verify surrounding functionality still works. Use a browser automation MCP server for verification. Record video and screenshots demonstrating the critical path succeeding. Then open a PR — unmerged PR is open, CI is confirmed green (use the wait-for-CI skill to block until CI passes or fails), and an independent fresh-eyes review of the PR has been performed and all of its feedback addressed (use wait-for-CI again after any fixes). Run that review through your runtime's in-process review path — the /code-review skill, Claude Code's native Task/Agent subagent, or Codex's native spawn_agent subagent — and do NOT spin up a separate Zimmer session solely to review your own PR (reserve start_session for genuinely independent downstream work). Ensure the PR title/description accurately reflects the final implementation. The PR description MUST include a '## Verification' section with checked boxes documenting how you closed the loop (CI green, tests added/passing, self-review done) AND proof that the change works. The verification section must explicitly state what the critical path is and include evidence (screenshots/video) of that specific path working. You must embed screenshots and video in the PR description — follow the capture-upload-embed procedure in GIT_WORKFLOW.md. Do NOT use unchecked checkboxes or a 'Test plan' with aspirational TODOs — every verification item must be completed before the PR is opened. Once the PR is open, CI is green, and self-review is complete, report the PR URL and a summary of what changed, then STOP and wait in needs_input state. Do NOT archive yourself — open sessions with unreviewed PRs serve as a visible todo-list for the user in the Zimmer UI. Only the user (or an explicit follow-up message) should trigger archiving after they have reviewed the PR.
